description.en: A 60+ year-old Michelin Bib Gourmand noodle institution in Sham Shui Po where third-generation owner Lau Fat-cheong hand-kneads dough with a heavy bamboo pole to create noodles with unmistakable springy, firm texture. Three generations of family expertise in one bowl.
description.zh-HK: 深水埗超過60年歷史的米芝蓮必比登麵家，第三代傳人劉法祥用竹竿壓麵，做出彈牙爽口的竹昇麵。三代家族手藝盡在一碗。

address.en: 48 Fuk Wing Street, Sham Shui Po, Kowloon, Hong Kong

tips: Order the shrimp wonton noodles — the signature dish that earned the Bib Gourmand
Arrive before noon to avoid the lunch queue; the shop is tiny with limited seating
Combine with a Sham Shui Po food crawl — Hop Yik Tai cheong fun is a 5-minute walk away

name.en: Oi Man Sang Dai Pai Dong

description.en: One of Hong Kong's oldest surviving dai pai dongs, operating in Sham Shui Po since 1956. This semi-outdoor street stall serves wok-hei-kissed Cantonese dishes with the kind of fierce heat only a seasoned dai pai dong wok can produce. Typhoon shelter crab and deep-fried squid are legendary.
description.zh-HK: 深水埗自1956年營業至今的老牌大排檔，半露天街頭食肆。鑊氣十足的廣東菜，避風塘炒蟹和炸魷魚堪稱一絕。

address.en: Kweilin Street, Sham Shui Po, Kowloon, Hong Kong

tips: Must-orders: typhoon shelter crab (HKD market price), deep fried squid (HKD138), stir-fried razor clams (HKD188)
Expect long queues after 7 PM — arrive at 6 PM for a table
The open-air setting means it's best on a cool evening; bring mosquito repellent in summer
Cash strongly preferred; Octopus card may be accepted

name.en: Tai Kwun Centre for Heritage and Arts

description.en: A beautifully restored complex of the former Central Police Station, Victoria Prison, and Central Magistracy — now Hong Kong's premier heritage-arts venue. Free lunchtime concerts, rotating contemporary art exhibitions, and excellent F&B outlets fill the grand Victorian courtyards.
description.zh-HK: 由前中區警署、域多利監獄及中央裁判司署活化而成的文化藝術中心。免費午間音樂會、當代藝術展覽和優質餐飲遍布維多利亞式庭院。

address.en: 10 Hollywood Road, Central, Hong Kong Island, Hong Kong

tips: Free admission to heritage buildings and most exhibitions — check their website for special shows
The free lunchtime concerts in the courtyard are a hidden highlight most visitors miss
Combine with PMQ (10-minute walk) for a full art-and-design afternoon in Central
Rooftop bar has stunning views of the surrounding skyscrapers

description.en: An immensely popular streetside dai pai dong in Central, founded in 1959. The tomato noodles — a deceptively simple dish of instant noodles in sweet-tangy tomato broth — have cult status. The crispy buns with lemon, butter, and honey are the perfect sweet finish.
description.zh-HK: 1959年創立的中環老牌街邊大排檔。茄汁公仔麵 — 簡單但令人上癮的番茄湯底即食麵 — 擁有狂熱追隨者。檸蜜脆脆多士是甜蜜收尾。

address.en: 2 Mei Lun Street, Central, Hong Kong Island, Hong Kong

tips: Tomato noodles (HK$30-40) are the must-order — don't overthink it, just get them
Open mornings only; usually closes by 5 PM, and closed on Sundays
Seating is communal metal stools on the sidewalk — this is old Hong Kong street dining at its purest
Near the Mid-Levels Escalator — combine with a PMQ and SoHo walk

description.en: A creative hub in a former police married quarters (built 1862 as Central School), now home to independent design boutiques, artisan workshops, and rotating art installations. Regular weekend markets and design pop-ups showcase Hong Kong's emerging creative talent.
description.zh-HK: 由前已婚警察宿舍活化的創意中心（原址為1862年中央書院），現為獨立設計店、工藝工作坊和藝術裝置的據點。週末定期市集展示香港新晉創意人才。

address.en: 35 Aberdeen Street, Central, Hong Kong Island, Hong Kong

tips: Weekend markets and pop-up events are the best time to visit — check their Instagram for schedules
The independent boutiques sell unique Hong Kong-designed products you won't find elsewhere
Free admission; some workshops and events may have a fee
Walk from here to Tai Kwun (10 min) via Hollywood Road for a combined art outing

description.en: A Michelin-recognized street stall in Sham Shui Po serving silky-smooth cheong fun (rice noodle rolls) made fresh daily. At just HK$10 for 4 pieces, dressed with soy sauce, sesame, sweet sauce, and chili, this is arguably the best-value Michelin bite in Hong Kong.
description.zh-HK: 深水埗米芝蓮推介的街頭小食，每日新鮮製作絲滑腸粉。四件只需港幣10元，配以醬油、芝麻、甜醬和辣醬，可能是全港最抵食的米芝蓮美食。

address.en: 121 Kweilin Street, Sham Shui Po, Kowloon, Hong Kong

tips: Best eaten fresh and hot — don't wait; eat immediately when served
Arrive before 9 AM on weekdays to avoid the queue
Perfect first stop on a Sham Shui Po food crawl — it's light enough to leave room for more
Exit C1 at Sham Shui Po MTR station, 3-minute walk

description.en: Asia's best urban hike (per Time Magazine), offering sweeping views of the South China Sea, Shek O, and Stanley from a ridge trail on Hong Kong Island's south side. Moderate difficulty, well-marked paths, and just 8.5 km — proving Hong Kong is far more than skyscrapers.
description.zh-HK: 《時代》雜誌評選亞洲最佳城市遠足徑，從港島南面山脊俯瞰南中國海、石澳和赤柱。難度中等，路徑清晰，全長8.5公里。

address.en: Shek O Road, Shek O, Hong Kong Island, Hong Kong

tips: Start early (7-8 AM) to beat the heat and get the best light for photos
Take Bus 9 from Shau Kei Wan MTR to the trailhead at To Tei Wan
End at Big Wave Bay for a swim and cold beer at the beach cafe
Bring 1.5L+ of water — there are no facilities on the trail itself

description.en: A peaceful heritage trail through Yuen Long's 600-year-old walled villages, ancestral halls, and pagodas — a window into Hong Kong's pre-colonial Hakka and Punti clan history that most visitors never see. Combine with local pineapple bun cafes and Yuen Long's legendary street food.
description.zh-HK: 元朗屏山600年歷史的圍村、祠堂和寶塔組成的寧靜文物徑，讓你看到殖民前客家和本地宗族的歷史。配合元朗的菠蘿包和街頭美食。

address.en: Ping Shan, Yuen Long, New Territories, Hong Kong

tips: Free admission to all heritage sites along the trail
After the trail, walk to Yuen Long town center for famous pineapple buns and milk tea
Combine with nearby Hong Kong Wetland Park for a full New Territories day trip
Take the West Rail Line to Tin Shui Wai station — the trail starts nearby
